title: Session 32: Ring on a String
content:
« Previous | Next »
Overview When Professor Jerison holds up a piece of string threaded through a ring, the ring slides to the lowest point possible. How would we use calculus to predict the location of that point? This is a minimization problem with an added twist: the position of the ring is constrained by the endpoints and length of the string.
Lecture Video and Notes Video Excerpts Clip 1: Ring on a String
